regime_justification: USDJPY approaching 160 watch line with MOF warning of 'decisive action'; BOJ likely on hold but inflation rising; spreads intact but intervention risk elevated.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 159.82, nearing 160 MOF watch line; Finance Minister Katayama warned of 'free hand to intervene' and readiness for 'decisive action'; vol still subdued but risk reversals unavailable; carry intact but fragile.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ meeting 27-28 Apr; USDJPY at intervention threshold; Katayama rhetoric escalating; spread ~186 bps still supports carry but MOF action risk rising.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ guidance critical; if BOJ signals June hike and USDJPY stays above 160, intervention probability jumps; oil still elevated at ~$95 WTI keeping yen pressure on.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Scenario-weighted: BOJ likely preps June hike, compressing forward spread expectations; Middle East uncertainty keeps energy-driven inflation risk alive; fragile setup persists.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If BOJ signals steady normalization path and oil retreats, carry can stabilize; MOF intervention would be one-off, not systemic unwind trigger unless sustained above 165.

bottom_line: USDJPY at 159.82 tests MOF red line; Katayama warned of 'decisive action,' BOJ meets 27-28 April likely signaling June hike. Carry spread ~186 bps intact but intervention risk elevated. Oil ~$95, core CPI 1.8% (ex-food/energy 2.4%). VIX 19.3, MOVE 67.7—complacency persists. Grades C/C/C/D: watchful near-term, unwind unlikely absent sustained >165 break or BOJ shock hawkishness. BTC ~$78k stable; crypto liquidity sympathy muted.

Market expects hold 27-28 Apr, June hike signal; surprise April hike ~10% probability but would trigger sharp unwind if delivered

trigger_map.trigger: USDJPY 1M risk reversals flipping to JPY-call premium
trigger_map.note: Data unavailable for Apr 24; historically flips near intervention; would confirm market pricing MOF action

narrative.p1: USDJPY climbed to 159.82 (+0.07% overnight), within 20 yen of the 160 MOF watch line. Finance Minister Katayama reiterated Japan retains a 'free hand to intervene' and stands ready for 'decisive action against speculative moves' at Bloomberg event Apr 23. Japan March core CPI rose to 1.8% YoY (first pickup in 5mo) but stayed below BOJ 2% target; core ex-food-energy 2.4% shows underlying pressure masked by fuel subsidies. WTI pulled back to $95.13 (-0.76%) after +17% week on Iran blockade hopes for diplomacy. No stage change—remains Stage 2 Pre-Intervention—but cocked.
narrative.p2: Carry mechanics: US-JP 10Y spread 186 bps (4.31% - 2.44%), compressed -6 bps as JGB yields spiked on energy inflation fears. USDJPY 1M implied vol estimated ~7.7% (MacroMicro Apr 17)—still subdued despite proximity to intervention zone, a complacency flag. Carry-to-vol proxy ~24 (186/7.7), healthy but compressing. USDJPY pace: 5d +0.41%, within normal range; MOF historically reacts to *pace* not level, so current drift tolerable but accelerating risk high if BOJ signals June hike 27-28 Apr. Computed ¥/bbl = 15,205 yen, elevated. Distance from 60d mean (157.3): ~160 bps stretched but not extreme. Grades C/C/C/D: watchful 1-3wk on BOJ meeting, intervention proximity, and oil; week 4 downgrades to D (unlikely) if BOJ normalizes guidance cleanly and oil retreats—unwind requires sustained >165 or shock, not single intervention. Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker between C and D for week 1-2; data genuinely ambiguous (intervention risk vs. carry spread intact)—would otherwise grade D absent Katayama rhetoric escalation.
narrative.p3: BTC held $77,736 (+0.45%), flat 1wk, resilient despite DXY 98.5 and USDJPY stress. No crypto sympathy unwind signal; BTC-DXY correlation data unavailable but price action suggests decoupling from FX vol. Open interest direction unclear (data missing). XRP $1.43 (+0.58%), similar stability. Interpretation: crypto liquidity still risk-on; if carry unwinds materially (Stage 4), expect BTC to drain toward $70-72k as levered positions close and DXY spikes. Current $77-78k consolidation neutral for crypto positioning—no urgency to de-risk, but no fresh bid either. Watch BTC reaction to BOJ 27-28 Apr for clues on whether crypto front-runs or lags carry unwind.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1-2 grades C (watchful): BOJ meeting 27-28 April is the catalyst. Consensus expects hold at 0.75% but hawkish signal for June hike. If BOJ revises inflation up (likely given Mar CPI and oil) and trims growth, forward spread expectations compress. USDJPY already at 159.82; any post-meeting drift toward 161+ invites MOF intervention. Katayama rhetoric escalated this week ('decisive action'), raising probability of action from ~30% to ~50% if 160 breached. VIX 19.3, MOVE 67.7—vol not pricing intervention risk. Grade reflects elevated but not imminent unwind risk; data-driven tiebreaker leaned one notch more alert per prior given ambiguous MOF posture.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2-3 grade C (watchful) extends: scenario-weighted for post-BOJ guidance digestion. If BOJ signals June hike (median economist view), carry forward curve steepens less attractively. Oil risk persists (Hormuz blockade unresolved); further WTI spike toward $100+ would force BOJ hand earlier. Key variable: does USDJPY stabilize 158-160 or break higher? Intervention would likely be one-off verbal/spot, not systemic unwind trigger, but increases fragility. Week 3 slightly elevated vs. week 2 because June meeting (15-16 June per BOJ calendar) comes into view—market begins pricing hike probability.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3-4 grade steps down C to D (unlikely): if BOJ delivers clean forward guidance (steady normalization, no shock hike), spread expectations stabilize. Oil diplomacy progress (Iran talks resuming per Apr 24 headlines) could pull WTI back to $85-90 range, easing yen import pressure and reducing BOJ urgency. MOF intervention, if it occurs, would be absorbed as policy tweak not regime shift unless USDJPY sustained >165 (far from current). Week 4 downgrades to D because absent a surprise catalyst (exogenous shock, BOJ April hike, Hormuz closure extension), the setup reverts to 'pressure building' (Stage 1) rather than acute unwind risk. Spread 186 bps still supports carry; VIX/MOVE complacent; equity bid intact.

watch_list: BOJ policy statement 28 April—language on June hike timing and inflation forecast revision
USDJPY daily close above 160.00—triggers high probability of MOF verbal or spot intervention within 24-48hr
WTI crude direction next 3 days—sustained move above $100 or retreat below $90 changes BOJ calculus materially
USD/JPY 1M risk reversals (when available)—flip to JPY-call premium would confirm market pricing intervention
Nikkei 225 reaction to BOJ—sharp selloff (>2%) would signal equity market pricing yen strength ahead, frontrunning unwind
